NKJV.1: Finally then, brethren, we urge and exhort in the Lord Jesus that you should abound more and more, just as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God;
NKJV.2: for you know what commandments we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
NKJV.3: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you should abstain from sexual immorality;
NKJV.4: that each of you should know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor,
NKJV.5: not in passion of lust, like the Gentiles who do not know God;
NKJV.6: that no one should take advantage of and defraud his brother in this matter, because the Lord is the avenger of all such, as we also forewarned you and testified.
NKJV.7: For God did not call us to uncleanness, but in holiness.
NKJV.8: Therefore he who rejects this does not reject man, but God, who has also given us His Holy Spirit.
NKJV.9: But concerning brotherly love you have no need that I should write to you, for you yourselves are taught by God to love one another;
NKJV.10: and indeed you do so toward all the brethren who are in all Macedonia. But we urge you, brethren, that you increase more and more;
NKJV.11: that you also aspire to lead a quiet life, to mind your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you,
NKJV.12: that you may walk properly toward those who are outside, and that you may lack nothing.
NKJV.13: But I do not want you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning those who have fallen asleep, lest you sorrow as others who have no hope.
NKJV.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with Him those who sleep in Jesus.
NKJV.15: For this we say to you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive and remain until the coming of the Lord will by no means precede those who are asleep.
NKJV.16: For the Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of an archangel, and with the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.
NKJV.17: Then we who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And thus we shall always be with the Lord.
NKJV.18: Therefore comfort one another with these words.

ESV.1: Finally, then, brothers,  we ask and urge you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God, just as you are doing, that you do so more and more.
ESV.2: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
ESV.3: For this is the will of God, your sanctification:  that you abstain from sexual immorality;
ESV.4: that each one of you know how to control his own body  in holiness and honor,
ESV.5: not in the passion of lust like the Gentiles who do not know God;
ESV.6: that no one transgress and wrong his brother in this matter, because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as we told you beforehand and solemnly warned you.
ESV.7: For God has not called us for impurity, but in holiness.
ESV.8: Therefore whoever disregards this, disregards not man but God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
ESV.9: Now concerning brotherly love you have no need for anyone to write to you, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love one another,
ESV.10: for that indeed is what you are doing to all the brothers throughout Macedonia. But we urge you, brothers, to do this more and more,
ESV.11: and to aspire to live quietly, and to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we instructed you,
ESV.12: so that you may walk properly before outsiders and be dependent on no one.
ESV.13: But we do not want you to be uninformed, brothers, about those who are asleep, that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope.
ESV.14: For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so, through Jesus, God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep.
ESV.15: For this we declare to you by a word from the Lord,  that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will not precede those who have fallen asleep.
ESV.16: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.
ESV.17: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.
ESV.18: Therefore encourage one another with these words.

NASB.1: Finally then, brethren, we request and exhort you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us [instruction] as to how you ought to walk and please God (just as you actually do walk), that you excel still more.
NASB.2: For you know what commandments we gave you by [the authority of] the Lord Jesus.
NASB.3: For this is the will of God, your sanctification; [that is], that you abstain from sexual immorality;
NASB.4: that each of you know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor,
NASB.5: not in lustful passion, like the Gentiles who do not know God;
NASB.6: [and] that no man transgress and defraud his brother in the matter because the Lord is [the] avenger in all these things, just as we also told you before and solemnly warned [you].
NASB.7: For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification.
NASB.8: So, he who rejects [this] is not rejecting man but the God who gives His Holy Spirit to you.
NASB.9: Now as to the love of the brethren, you have no need for [anyone] to write to you, for you yourselves are taught by God to love one another;
NASB.10: for indeed you do practice it toward all the brethren who are in all Macedonia. But we urge you, brethren, to excel still more,
NASB.11: and to make it your ambition to lead a quiet life and attend to your own business and work with your hands, just as we commanded you,
NASB.12: so that you will behave properly toward outsiders and not be in any need.
NASB.13: But we do not want you to be uninformed, brethren, about those who are asleep, so that you will not grieve as do the rest who have no hope.
NASB.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with Him those who have fallen asleep in Jesus.
NASB.15: For this we say to you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive and remain until the coming of the Lord, will not precede those who have fallen asleep.
NASB.16: For the Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of [the] archangel and with the trumpet of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
NASB.17: Then we who are alive and remain will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we shall always be with the Lord.
NASB.18: Therefore comfort one another with these words.

NLT.1: Finally, dear brothers and sisters, we urge you in the name of the Lord Jesus to live in a way that pleases God, as we have taught you. You live this way already, and we encourage you to do so even more.
NLT.2: For you remember what we taught you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
NLT.3: God’s will is for you to be holy, so stay away from all sexual sin.
NLT.4: Then each of you will control his own body and live in holiness and honor —
NLT.5: not in lustful passion like the pagans who do not know God and his ways.
NLT.6: Never harm or cheat a fellow believer in this matter by violating his wife, for the Lord avenges all such sins, as we have solemnly warned you before.
NLT.7: God has called us to live holy lives, not impure lives.
NLT.8: Therefore, anyone who refuses to live by these rules is not disobeying human teaching but is rejecting God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
NLT.9: But we don’t need to write to you about the importance of loving each other, for God himself has taught you to love one another.
NLT.10: Indeed, you already show your love for all the believers throughout Macedonia. Even so, dear brothers and sisters, we urge you to love them even more.
NLT.11: Make it your goal to live a quiet life, minding your own business and working with your hands, just as we instructed you before.
NLT.12: Then people who are not believers will respect the way you live, and you will not need to depend on others.
NLT.13: And now, dear brothers and sisters, we want you to know what will happen to the believers who have died so you will not grieve like people who have no hope.
NLT.14: For since we believe that Jesus died and was raised to life again, we also believe that when Jesus returns, God will bring back with him the believers who have died.
NLT.15: We tell you this directly from the Lord: We who are still living when the Lord returns will not meet him ahead of those who have died.
NLT.16: For the Lord himself will come down from heaven with a commanding shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trumpet call of God. First, the believers who have died will rise from their graves.
NLT.17: Then, together with them, we who are still alive and remain on the earth will be caught up in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. Then we will be with the Lord forever.
NLT.18: So encourage each other with these words.

WEB.1: Finally then, brothers, we beg and exhort you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God, that you abound more and more.
WEB.2: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
WEB.3: For this is the will of God: your sanctification, that you abstain from sexual immorality,
WEB.4: that each one of you know how to control his own body  in sanctification and honor,
WEB.5: not in the passion of lust, even as the Gentiles who don’t know God;
WEB.6: that no one should take advantage of and wrong a brother or sister in this matter; because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as also we forewarned you and testified.
WEB.7: For God called us not for uncleanness, but in sanctification.
WEB.8: Therefore he who rejects this doesn’t reject man, but God, who has also given his Holy Spirit to you.
WEB.9: But concerning brotherly love, you have no need that one write to you. For you yourselves are taught by God to love one another,
WEB.10: for indeed you do it toward all the brothers who are in all Macedonia. But we exhort you, brothers, that you abound more and more;
WEB.11: and that you make it your ambition to lead a quiet life, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, even as we instructed you;
WEB.12: that you may walk properly toward those who are outside, and may have need of nothing.
WEB.13: But we don’t want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep, so that you don’t grieve like the rest, who have no hope.
WEB.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep in Jesus.
WEB.15: For this we tell you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left to the coming of the Lord, will in no way precede those who have fallen asleep.
WEB.16: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with God’s trumpet. The dead in Christ will rise first,
WEB.17: then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air. So we will be with the Lord forever.
WEB.18: Therefore comfort one another with these words.

CSB17.1: Additionally then, brothers and sisters, we ask and encourage you in the Lord Jesus, that as you have received instruction from us on how you should live and please God — as you are doing <sup>[1]</sup>  — do this even more.
CSB17.2: For you know what commands we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
CSB17.3: For this is God’s will, <sup>ⓘ</sup> your sanctification: <sup>ⓙ</sup> that you keep away <sup>ⓚ</sup> from sexual immorality,
CSB17.4: that each of you knows how to control his own body <sup>[2]</sup> in holiness and honor,
CSB17.5: not with lustful passions, <sup>ⓛ</sup> like the Gentiles, who don’t know God.
CSB17.6: This means one must not transgress against and take advantage <sup>ⓜ</sup> of a brother or sister in this manner, because the Lord is an avenger of all these offenses, as we also previously told and warned you.
CSB17.7: For God has not called us to impurity <sup>ⓝ</sup> but to live in holiness.
CSB17.8: Consequently, anyone who rejects this does not reject man, but God, who gives you his Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.9: About brotherly love: You don’t need me to write you because you yourselves are taught by God <sup>ⓑ</sup> to love one another. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.10: In fact, you are doing this toward all the brothers and sisters in the entire region of Macedonia. <sup>ⓓ</sup> But we encourage you, brothers and sisters, to do this even more,
CSB17.11: to seek to lead a quiet life, <sup>ⓔ</sup> to mind your own business, <sup>[3]</sup> and to work <sup>ⓕ</sup> with your own hands, as we commanded you,
CSB17.12: so that you may behave properly <sup>ⓖ</sup> in the presence of outsiders and not be dependent on anyone. <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.13: We do not want you to be uninformed, brothers and sisters, concerning those who are asleep, so that you will not grieve like the rest, who have no hope. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose <sup>ⓙ</sup> again, <sup>ⓚ</sup> in the same way, through Jesus, <sup>ⓛ</sup> God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep.
CSB17.15: For we say this to you by a word from the Lord: <sup>ⓜ</sup> We who are still alive at the Lord’s coming <sup>ⓝ</sup> will certainly not precede those who have fallen asleep. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.16: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a shout, <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> with the archangel’s <sup>ⓒ</sup> voice, and with the trumpet of God, and the dead in Christ <sup>ⓓ</sup> will rise first.
CSB17.17: Then <sup>ⓔ</sup> we who are still alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds <sup>ⓕ</sup> to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.
CSB17.18: Therefore encourage <sup>[6]</sup> one another with these words.

AMP.1: Finally, believers, we ask and admonish you in the Lord Jesus, that you follow the instruction that you received from us about how you ought to walk and please God (just as you are actually doing) and that you excel even more and more [pursuing a life of purpose and living in a way that expresses gratitude to God for your salvation].
AMP.2: For you know what commandments and precepts we gave you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
AMP.3: For this is the will of God, that you be sanctified [separated and set apart from sin]: that you abstain and back away from sexual immorality;
AMP.4: that each of you know how to control his own body in holiness and honor [being available for God's purpose and separated from things profane],
AMP.5: not [to be used] in lustful passion, like the Gentiles who do not know God and are ignorant of His will;
AMP.6: and that [in this matter of sexual misconduct] no man shall transgress and defraud his brother because the Lord is the avenger in all these things, just as we have told you before and solemnly warned you.
AMP.7: For God has not called us to impurity, but to holiness [to be dedicated, and set apart by behavior that pleases Him, whether in public or in private].
AMP.8: So whoever rejects and disregards this is not [merely] rejecting man but the God who gives His Holy Spirit to you [to dwell in you and empower you to overcome temptation].
AMP.9: Now concerning brotherly love, you have no need for anyone to write you, for you have been [personally] taught by God to love one another [that is, to have an unselfish concern for others and to do things for their benefit].
AMP.10: For indeed you already do practice it toward all the believers throughout Macedonia [by actively displaying your love and concern for them]. But we urge you, brothers and sisters, that you excel [in this matter] more and more,
AMP.11: and to make it your ambition to live quietly and peacefully, and to mind your own affairs and work with your hands, just as we directed you,
AMP.12: so that you will behave properly toward outsiders [exhibiting good character, personal integrity, and moral courage worthy of the respect of the outside world], and be dependent on no one and in need of nothing [be self-supporting].
AMP.13: Now we do not want you to be uninformed, believers, about those who are asleep [in death], so that you will not grieve [for them] as the others do who have no hope [beyond this present life].
AMP.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again [as in fact He did], even so God [in this same way—by raising them from the dead] will bring with Him those [believers] who have fallen asleep in Jesus.
AMP.15: For we say this to you by the Lord's [own] word, that we who are still alive and remain until the coming of the Lord, will in no way precede [into His presence] those [believers] who have fallen asleep [in death].
AMP.16: For the Lord Himself will come down from heaven with a shout of command, with the voice of the archangel and with the [blast of the] trumpet of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
AMP.17: Then we who are alive and remain [on the earth] will simultaneously be caught up (raptured) together with them [the resurrected ones] in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord!
AMP.18: Therefore comfort and encourage one another with these words [concerning our reunion with believers who have died].

MSG.1: One final word, friends. We ask you—urge is more like it—that you keep on doing what we told you to do to please God, not in a dogged religious plod, but in a living, spirited dance.
MSG.2: You know the guidelines we laid out for you from the Master Jesus.
MSG.3: God wants you to live a pure life. Keep yourselves from sexual promiscuity.
MSG.4: Learn to appreciate and give dignity to your body,
MSG.5: not abusing it, as is so common among those who know nothing of God.
MSG.6: Don't run roughshod over the concerns of your brothers and sisters. Their concerns are God's concerns, and he will take care of them. We've warned you about this before.
MSG.7: God hasn't invited us into a disorderly, unkempt life but into something holy and beautiful—as beautiful on the inside as the outside.
MSG.8: If you disregard this advice, you're not offending your neighbors; you're rejecting God, who is making you a gift of his Holy Spirit.
MSG.9: Regarding life together and getting along with each other, you don't need me to tell you what to do. You're God-taught in these matters. Just love one another!
MSG.10: You're already good at it; your friends all over the province of Macedonia are the evidence. Keep it up; get better and better at it.
MSG.11: Stay calm; mind your own business; do your own job. You've heard all this from us before, but a reminder never hurts.
MSG.12: We want you living in a way that will command the respect of outsiders, not lying around sponging off your friends.
MSG.13: And regarding the question, friends, that has come up about what happens to those already dead and buried, we don't want you in the dark any longer. First off, you must not carry on over them like people who have nothing to look forward to, as if the grave were the last word.
MSG.14: Since Jesus died and broke loose from the grave, God will most certainly bring back to life those who died in Jesus.
MSG.15: And then this: We can tell you with complete confidence—we have the Master's word on it—that when the Master comes again to get us, those of us who are still alive will not get a jump on the dead and leave them behind. In actual fact, they'll be ahead of us.
MSG.16: The Master himself will give the command. Archangel thunder! God's trumpet blast! He'll come down from heaven and the dead in Christ will rise—they'll go first.
MSG.17: Then the rest of us who are still alive at the time will be caught up with them into the clouds to meet the Master.
MSG.18: Oh, we'll be walking on air! And then there will be one huge family reunion with the Master. So reassure one another with these words.

NIV.1:
Living to Please God
Finally, brothers, we instructed you how to live in order to please God, as in fact you are living. Now we ask you and urge you in the Lord Jesus to do this more and more.
NIV.2: For you know what instructions we gave you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
NIV.3: It is God’s will that you should be sanctified: that you should avoid sexual immorality;
NIV.4: that each of you should learn to control his own body in a way that is holy and honorable,
NIV.5: not in passionate lust like the heathen, who do not know God;
NIV.6: and that in this matter no one should wrong his brother or take advantage of him. The Lord will punish men for all such sins, as we have already told you and warned you.
NIV.7: For God did not call us to be impure, but to live a holy life.
NIV.8: Therefore, he who rejects this instruction does not reject man but God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
NIV.9: Now about brotherly love we do not need to write to you, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love each other.
NIV.10: And in fact, you do love all the brothers throughout Macedonia. Yet we urge you, brothers, to do so more and more.
NIV.11: Make it your ambition to lead a quiet life, to mind your own business and to work with your hands, just as we told you,
NIV.12: so that your daily life may win the respect of outsiders and so that you will not be dependent on anybody.
NIV.13:
The Coming of the Lord
Brothers, we do not want you to be ignorant about those who fall asleep, or to grieve like the rest of men, who have no hope.
NIV.14: We believe that Jesus died and rose again and so we believe that God will bring with Jesus those who have fallen asleep in him.
NIV.15: According to the Lord’s own word, we tell you that we who are still alive, who are left till the coming of the Lord, will certainly not precede those who have fallen asleep.
NIV.16: For the Lord himself will come down from heaven, with a loud command, with the voice of the archangel and with the trumpet call of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
NIV.17: After that, we who are still alive and are left will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And so we will be with the Lord forever.
NIV.18: Therefore encourage each other with these words.

NRSVCE.1: Finally, brothers and sisters, we ask and urge you in the Lord Jesus that, as you learned from us how you ought to live and to please God (as, in fact, you are doing), you should do so more and more.
NRSVCE.2: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
NRSVCE.3: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you abstain from fornication;
NRSVCE.4: that each one of you know how to control your own body in holiness and honor,
NRSVCE.5: not with lustful passion, like the Gentiles who do not know God;
NRSVCE.6: that no one wrong or exploit a brother or sister in this matter, because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, just as we have already told you beforehand and solemnly warned you.
NRSVCE.7: For God did not call us to impurity but in holiness.
NRSVCE.8: Therefore whoever rejects this rejects not human authority but God, who also gives his Holy Spirit to you.
NRSVCE.9: Now concerning love of the brothers and sisters, you do not need to have anyone write to you, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love one another;
NRSVCE.10: and indeed you do love all the brothers and sisters throughout Macedonia. But we urge you, beloved, to do so more and more,
NRSVCE.11: to aspire to live quietly, to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we directed you,
NRSVCE.12: so that you may behave properly toward outsiders and be dependent on no one.
NRSVCE.13: But we do not want you to be uninformed, brothers and sisters, about those who have died, so that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope.
NRSVCE.14: For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so, through Jesus, God will bring with him those who have died.
NRSVCE.15: For this we declare to you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will by no means precede those who have died.
NRSVCE.16: For the Lord himself, with a cry of command, with the archangel’s call and with the sound of God’s trumpet, will descend from heaven, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
NRSVCE.17: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up in the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the air; and so we will be with the Lord forever.
NRSVCE.18: Therefore encourage one another with these words.

NET.1: Finally then, brothers and sisters, we ask you and urge you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received instruction from us about how you must live and please God (as you are in fact living) that you do so more and more.
NET.2: For you know what commands we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
NET.3: For this is God’s will: that you become holy, that you keep away from sexual immorality,
NET.4: that each of you know how to possess his own body in holiness and honor,
NET.5: not in lustful passion like the Gentiles who do not know God.
NET.6: In this matter no one should violate the rights of his brother or take advantage of him, because the Lord is the avenger in all these cases, as we also told you earlier and warned you solemnly.
NET.7: For God did not call us to impurity but in holiness.
NET.8: Consequently the one who rejects this is not rejecting human authority but God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
NET.9: Now on the topic of brotherly love you have no need for anyone to write you, for you yourselves are taught by God to love one another.
NET.10: And indeed you are practicing it toward all the brothers and sisters in all of Macedonia. But we urge you, brothers and sisters, to do so more and more,
NET.11: to aspire to lead a quiet life, to attend to your own business, and to work with your hands, as we commanded you.
NET.12: In this way you will live a decent life before outsiders and not be in need.
NET.13: Now we do not want you to be uninformed, brothers and sisters, about those who are asleep, so that you will not grieve like the rest who have no hope.
NET.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, so also we believe that God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep as Christians.
NET.15: For we tell you this by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will surely not go ahead of those who have fallen asleep.
NET.16: For the Lord himself will come down from heaven with a shout of command, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trumpet of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.
NET.17: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be suddenly caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And so we will always be with the Lord.
NET.18: Therefore encourage one another with these words.
